skill.name: polish
skill.description: Final quality pass before shipping. Fixes alignment, spacing, consistency, and detail issues that separate good from great.
skill.summary: Final quality pass for UI. Fixes alignment, spacing, consistency, and detail issues.
skill.author: pbakaus

security_audit.summary: All 18 static findings are false positives. The scanner incorrectly flagged design/UI terminology (grid, alignment, tokens, responsiveness) as security issues. No actual code execution, cryptographic algorithms, or reconnaissance patterns exist. This is a legitimate polish skill with no security risks.

content.value_statement: A meticulous quality pass skill that catches alignment, spacing, consistency, and detail issues in UI code. Transforms good work into polished, professional interfaces.
actual_capabilities: Systematically review visual alignment and spacing against design tokens
Verify all interactive element states (hover, focus, active, disabled, loading, error)
Check typography hierarchy, line lengths, and readability standards
Ensure WCAG accessibility compliance for contrast and focus indicators
Validate responsive behavior across all breakpoints
Clean up code by removing console logs, unused imports, and commented code
limitations: Cannot execute code or run tests - only provides guidance
Does not have visual output - cannot see rendered UI
Cannot modify files without explicit user permission
Does not have access to design files or Figma

use_cases.title: Final UI Review Before Launch
use_cases.description: Use before shipping a feature to catch spacing inconsistencies, missing states, and accessibility issues.
use_cases.target_user: Frontend developers preparing features for production

use_cases.title: Code Quality Improvement
use_cases.description: Clean up console logs, unused imports, and ensure consistent naming conventions.
use_cases.target_user: Developers maintaining codebases who want higher quality

use_cases.title: Accessibility Audit
use_cases.description: Verify focus indicators, contrast ratios, and proper ARIA labels are present.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Polish Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the polish skill to review the recent changes in this file. What issues should I address before shipping?

prompt_templates.title: Focus on Accessibility
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the polish skill and focus on accessibility. Check for focus indicators, contrast, and ARIA labels.

prompt_templates.title: Code Cleanup Only
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the polish skill but focus only on code quality: remove console logs, unused imports, and clean up dead code.

prompt_templates.title: Full Component Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the polish skill to do a full review of this component. Check alignment, spacing, all states, and accessibility.

output_examples.input: Polish this button component before shipping
output_examples.output:
Here are the polish issues to address:

**Visual Alignment & Spacing**
- Button padding is 8px 12px but design tokens specify 8px 16px
- Vertical alignment with icon is off by 2px (optical adjustment needed)

**Interaction States**
- Missing disabled state (add opacity: 0.5 and cursor: not-allowed)
- Hover transition should be 150ms, currently instant

**Code Quality**
- Remove console.log on line 23
- Import 'disabled' prop is unused

**Accessibility**
- Add aria-label for icon-only button

Polish findings for form validation:

**Error States**
- Error messages are not consistently placed (some inline, some below field)
- Error text color #999 fails WCAG AA contrast (needs #d32f2f or darker)

**Success States**
- Missing success state for valid inputs (green border + checkmark)

**Labels & Validation**
- 'Required' indicator inconsistent (asterisk vs 'Required' text)
- Validation timing mixes on-blur and on-submit

**Focus Indicators**
- Focus ring removed on line 45 without replacement

best_practices: Complete functional work before polishing - polish is the last step, not the first
Use browser DevTools grid overlay to verify alignment visually
Test on real devices, not just responsive mode in browser
anti_patterns: Polishing code that will be thrown away or significantly changed
Spending hours on polish when the feature ships in 30 minutes
Perfecting one part while leaving others rough - maintain consistent quality level

faq.question: Does this skill modify my code?
faq.answer: No. The polish skill provides recommendations only. It will not modify any files without your explicit permission.

faq.question: Can it check accessibility?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks for WCAG contrast, focus indicators, ARIA labels, and keyboard navigation. Run with 'focus on accessibility' for detailed checks.

faq.question: Does it work with any framework?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is framework-agnostic. It checks CSS, HTML, JavaScript, and TypeScript for quality issues.

faq.question: How long should I spend on polish?
faq.answer: It depends on your timeline. For a 30-minute ship, do minimal polish. For a flagship feature, be thorough. The skill helps you triage.

faq.question: Can it find console.log statements?
faq.answer: Yes. The code quality section explicitly checks for console.log, debug comments, unused imports, and dead code.

faq.question: Does it check design tokens?
faq.answer: Yes. It verifies that colors, spacing, and typography use design tokens rather than hard-coded values.
